title: Corethrogyne incana
intro: Corethrogyne incana, also known as Gray Samosa or California Yerba Santa, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the coastal regions of California and Baja California in Mexico. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as bronchitis, fever, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Corethrogyne incana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make tea, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and as a general tonic to promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Corethrogyne incana is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is commonly known as desert everlasting or California everlasting. This perennial herb grows from a woody base and produces small, white flowers that resemble daisies. Corethrogyne incana is often found in dry, sandy areas and is tolerant of drought conditions. As a member of the Asteraceae family, it is closely related to other plants such as sunflowers and dandelions.
